phase0_sources_failed: src-7canibales-luchini (no guide-format article with Luchini byline in year range)
src-gastroactitud-capel (all articles use editorial 'we'; Capel byline not confirmed)
meta.note_infatuation_truncated: Jina fetch returned only 11 of ~30 Infatuation entries; additional both-pool entries may exist in full article
meta.note_estimar_compressed: Estimar (both-pool) accepted from Richardson; AI summary compressed quote to 'shellfish temple'; original article likely provides full qualifying quote

sources.writer_profile: Paul Richardson is a British food writer based in Spain; author of multiple books on Spanish food and wine; longtime Epicurean Ways columnist
⚠ coi — Time Out operates Time Out Market Madrid, a commercial food hall. Gorka Elorrieta is the confirmed named author for Time Out Madrid restaurant coverage
⚠ coi — Lauren Aloise is co-founder of Devour Tours, which operates food tours in Madrid. Single-author site; all articles attributed to Lauren Aloise
Lori Zaino is The Infatuation's Madrid editor; confirmed named byline on Madrid restaurant coverage
Mapi Hermida is the founder and sole author of La Gastrónoma; single-author site confirmed via About page
Carlos Osorio is a named contributor to eldiario.es Somos Madrid section; independent journalism covering Madrid food culture
Julián Acebes is the named author on GastroMadrid neighbourhood restaurant guides; confirmed byline on this Chamberí article
Felicity Hughes is the founder and sole author of The Making of Madrid; single-author site confirmed via About page
Brenda Alonso is a named contributor to The Objective gastronomy section; confirmed byline on this Repsol 2025 Madrid guide

restaurants.name: Angelita
restaurants.review_quote: Villalón brothers' evolved bar-café open Monday to Friday from 5:30pm; operating as a new gastronomic model with wine-led menu and rotating list

restaurants.name: Casa Labra
restaurants.review_quote: Historic Madrid tapas bar famous as the birthplace of the Spanish Socialist Party in 1879; known for vermouth and salt cod croquettes that draw locals daily

restaurants.name: Casa Salvador
restaurants.review_quote: Atmospheric 1941 interior with chequered tablecloths and bullfight memorabilia; known for kidneys in sherry, chicken in almond sauce, and classic madrileño recipes

restaurants.name: Estimar
restaurants.review_quote: Rafa Zafra's minimalist approach of three to four ingredients per plate; Mediterranean seafood sourced from chef Anna Gotanegra's fishing family

restaurants.name: Haramboure
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Patxi Zumárraga earning a Repsol Sol in 2025; features Bizkaia vegetables, Cantabrian fish, selected meats, known for tarta tatín de cebollas

restaurants.name: Kappo
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Mario Payán with no fixed menu; daily preparations before 20 diners on a minimalist wooden bar emphasising absolute respect for the product

restaurants.name: La Fisna
restaurants.review_quote: Wine bar with a 37-page wine list; features Spanish cheeses, glazed meatballs, egg omelet with Catalan sobrasada, and Basque piparras peppers

restaurants.name: La Tasquita de Enfrente
restaurants.review_quote: Near-cult status through owner Juanjo López's emphasis on top-notch raw materials; baby peas with ibérico bacon described as a luxury greater than caviar

restaurants.name: La Venencia
restaurants.review_quote: Nearly 100-year-old sherry bar where Hemingway found inspiration; serves only sherry with olives, chorizo, and cheese — no menus, just a small sign on the wall

restaurants.name: Lakasa
restaurants.address: Plaza del Descubridor Diego de Ordás, 1
restaurants.review_quote: César Martín's destination for Wellington roast served on select days; guiso specialities like verdinas and desserts that finish the meal memorably

restaurants.name: Lana
restaurants.review_quote: Brothers Martín and Joaquín Narváiz earning a Repsol Sol promotion in 2025; premium cuts — Aberdeen Angus, Wagyu, Galician beef — alongside empanadas and offal in warm elegant surroundings

restaurants.name: Lhardy
restaurants.review_quote: One of Madrid's most historic restaurants, established 1839; known for puff pastries, sole with champagne, steak tartare, and consommé served from a silver samovar in the tienda bar

restaurants.name: Pilar Akaneya
restaurants.review_quote: Madrid's premier sumibiyaki featuring certified Kobe beef, traditional Japanese charcoal, and minimalist architecture; earned Best Madrid Restaurant recognition

restaurants.name: Ramón Freixa Tradición
restaurants.review_quote: Freixa's accessible concept split from the tasting-menu Atelier; recognisable dishes served at uninterrupted hours in the same Salamanca address

restaurants.name: Sacha
restaurants.review_quote: Laidback bistro run by host Sacha Hormaechea with one of Madrid's prettiest terraces; personality-driven cooking with a loyal local following

restaurants.name: Sala de Despiece
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Javier Bonet's 50-square-metre tapas temple decorated as an authentic butcher shop with hand-written menu and a special 12-seat private counter

restaurants.name: Soy Kitchen
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Yong Ping Zhang blends Asian cuisine with Mediterranean and Latin touches, emphasising daily-selected premium Spanish products alongside classic wok cooking

restaurants.name: Tripea
restaurants.review_quote: Roberto Martínez Foronda's fusion of Asian, Peruvian, and traditional Spanish bases; Bib Gourmand and Repsol Sol recipient featuring causa limeña with Iberian jowl veil

restaurants.name: Verdejo
restaurants.review_quote: Verdejo Taberna Artesana earning Repsol Sol promotion in 2025; rotating seasonal menu with guiso de pochas con codorniz and alcachofas confitadas con jamón ibérico

restaurants.name: Allégorie
restaurants.address: Bretón de los Herreros, Ríos Rosas
restaurants.review_quote: Two-level French dining: ground floor for casual à la carte, upstairs for a fixed-menu tasting room; bistro and gastronomic in one address

restaurants.name: Asturianos
restaurants.review_quote: Modest exterior concealing genuine quality; brothers Alberto and Belarmino Fernández run the front while mother Julia cooks steaming bowls of perfect fabada asturiana

restaurants.name: Bacira
restaurants.review_quote: Led by chefs Carlos Langreo, Vicente de la Red, and Gabriel Zapata; merges Mediterranean, Japanese, and Nikkei traditions in a shareable menu format

restaurants.name: Bakko
restaurants.review_quote: Founded by food critic and influencer Alberto de Luna; described as Madrid's biggest restaurant hit of the season, offering haute Japanese cuisine with an exceptional wine list

restaurants.name: Bar Manchego
restaurants.review_quote: Specialises in tortilla with creative fillings; noted as some of the best tortilla in Madrid despite its casual neighbourhood setting

restaurants.name: Bar la Campana
restaurants.review_quote: My pick for Madrid's best calamari sandwich; always has the longest queue at Christmas and provides a truly local experience off the Plaza Mayor

restaurants.name: Bascoat
restaurants.review_quote: Rodrigo García and Nagore Irazuegi elevate New Basque cuisine without pretension; grilled specialities emphasised throughout the menu

restaurants.name: Bing Huo Bar
restaurants.review_quote: Specialises in hand-torn biang biang noodles; the beef version is particularly outstanding and worth the journey to Usera alone

restaurants.name: Biri Biri
restaurants.review_quote: Argentine-inflected tavern prioritising flavour without excess; an accessible address that delivers good cooking in an easygoing setting

restaurants.name: Bodega de la Ardosa
restaurants.review_quote: Historic tavern from 1892; standing-room-only atmosphere with day-drinking locals and abuelos; the front is where everyone hangs out with friends

restaurants.name: Bolboreta
restaurants.review_quote: Patricia Grandío and Aaron Quaife's intimate ten-table debut; honest, semiformal dining with DIY-refinished wood aesthetic

restaurants.name: Brutalista
restaurants.review_quote: Pablo's cinegetic masterwork; a small codorniz-manzanilla creation triggered the decade-long concept; dramatic lighting matches the intensity of the cooking

restaurants.name: Caja de Cerillas
restaurants.review_quote: Intimate spot by chef Enrique Valentí (formerly Barcelona-based) offering creative small plates from an open kitchen; nostalgic grandmother cooking reimagined

restaurants.name: Calvero
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Juanjo López venue within Gran Madrid Casino; famous for a seven-turn cocido on Thursdays that has become a ritual for regulars

restaurants.name: Casa Alberto
restaurants.review_quote: 19th-century tavern serving traditional Madrid cuisine; one of the oldest continuously operating tabernas in the city

restaurants.name: Casa Amadeo
restaurants.review_quote: Run by Amadeo Lázaro, who at 96 is one of Madrid's oldest active bar owners; celebrated for snails and hardcore madrileño dishes few other bars still serve

restaurants.name: Casa Dani
restaurants.review_quote: Inside Mercado de la Paz in Salamanca; serves one of my favourite tortillas in the city and a famous menú del día with traditional dishes

restaurants.name: Casa Lucas
restaurants.review_quote: Tiny, cosy La Latina tapas bar with great wines by the glass; delicious food and small enough that reservations are recommended

restaurants.name: Casa Mingo
restaurants.review_quote: Former railway warehouse serving roasted chicken and Asturian cider; spicy chorizo marinated in sidra and Cabrales blue cheese on the menu

restaurants.name: Casa Mortero
restaurants.review_quote: Pedro Gallego elevates humble dishes with precision; patatas a la importancia served in their essential form without empty garnish

restaurants.name: Casa Paulino
restaurants.review_quote: Traditional cuisine with contemporary touches since 1954; dishes include salmon with grape sauce and morcilla lasagna

restaurants.name: Casa Revuelta
restaurants.review_quote: The best place in town to try cod fritters; the move is to order dos bacalaos and wash it down with a beer for a quick authentic stop

restaurants.name: Casa Toni
restaurants.review_quote: Heaven for lovers of the authentic; famous for organ meats but the fried eggplant with honey, padrón peppers, and fried baby squid are unmissable

restaurants.name: Celso y Manolo
restaurants.review_quote: One of my favourite restaurants in all of Madrid; mostly organic food with casual ambience; beefsteak tomato salad and red prawns are standouts

restaurants.name: Chitón
restaurants.review_quote: Reinterprets castiza cuisine with sophistication and includes an underground nightclub as a second act to the evening

restaurants.name: Dantxari
restaurants.review_quote: Basque home cooking since 1997 featuring solomillo, rape, bacalao, alcachofas, and anchoas; faithful to the northern tradition

restaurants.name: Desborre
restaurants.review_quote: Lucia's modern bistro converting traditional Rioja recipes for a Madrid audience; located in the former Algarabía space

restaurants.name: El Anciano Rey de los Vinos
restaurants.review_quote: Located across from the Royal Palace; famous for traditional dishes like bull tail and huevos rotos; serves vermouth and red wines

restaurants.name: El Gran Asador Lecanda
restaurants.review_quote: Specialises in grilled meats including chuletón de vaca rubia gallega and grilled octopus; earning a Repsol Sol in the 2025 guide

restaurants.name: El Guisandar
restaurants.review_quote: Stewed dishes and broths prepared with traditional reverence; a dedicated address for slow-cooked guisos done with care

restaurants.name: El Lince
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Javi Estévez's expansion offering cocina castiza, directa y sin filtros at a more accessible price point than La Tasquería

restaurants.name: El Señor Martín
restaurants.review_quote: Started as a fishmonger and expanded into a full restaurant with a laudable policy of supporting sustainable fishing; product-led seafood cooking

restaurants.name: El Sobrino de Botín
restaurants.review_quote: Probably Madrid's most famous restaurant and the oldest in the world, open since 1725; specialty is roast suckling pig with underground wine caves

restaurants.name: El Ñeru
restaurants.review_quote: One of the most famous Asturian restaurants in the city centre; the daily lunch menu is beloved, especially the hearty fabada in winter

restaurants.name: Emma Cocina
restaurants.review_quote: Tapas bar near Plaza Mayor where locals and tourists mix; features stewed pork cheek, cured lomo, chorizo, and Spanish cheeses served on olive oil with picos

restaurants.name: Fanáticos del Sabor
restaurants.review_quote: Incredible value at under €10 for a hearty lunch; features chaulafán fried rice on a welcoming terrace in the heart of Usera

restaurants.name: Fismuler
restaurants.review_quote: Patxi Zumárraga's casual-industrial space featuring regional cuisine with Basque-Navarrese influences and contemporary touches, born from elBulli experience

restaurants.name: Gamberro Taberna Canalla
restaurants.review_quote: Shellfish-focused tavern with creative presentations and signature fried potatoes at a lively Olavide plaza address

restaurants.name: Gaudium
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Juan Carlos Peñas presents simple traditional cooking with innovative touches; from classic puchero to pork sirloin with cocoa and saffron

restaurants.name: Helaotai
restaurants.review_quote: Excels at noodle soups with an amazing tripe salad and large, crispy-bottomed dumplings that have made it a neighbourhood favourite

restaurants.name: Ikigai
restaurants.review_quote: Yong Wu Nagahira's reflective Japanese fusion with quality product and relaxed service; chawan mushi compresses the menu's conceptual appeal

restaurants.name: Katsu
restaurants.review_quote: Japanese fried-food temple: sandwiches, rice bowls, and reinterpretations like smash gyoza; quality Japanese fast food done with care

restaurants.name: Kentya
restaurants.review_quote: Transparent cooking celebrating Mediterranean tradition with a contemporary approach; product-focused and market-driven in Salamanca

restaurants.name: Kitchen 154
restaurants.review_quote: Authentic tasca using 90% market-sourced products; specialising in Korean BBQ ribs cooked 12 hours with gochujang sauce and homemade kimchi

restaurants.name: Kuoco
restaurants.review_quote: Venezuelan-led fusion restaurant elevated to a new level after relocating; combines Asian, Peruvian, Mexican, and Indian flavours into a coherent creative menu

restaurants.name: Kyoshi Las Cortes
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Ricardo Sanz leads a refined sushi bar with sashimi de toro con caviar and a curated sake and wine selection earning a Repsol Sol in 2025

restaurants.name: La Alquimia
restaurants.review_quote: Natural wine bar founded after a wedding celebration; offering honest selections alongside good charcuterie in a relaxed Chamberí setting

restaurants.name: La Berenjena
restaurants.review_quote: Owner appeared on Spanish MasterChef; one of Madrid's most delicious food spots with a recently opened second location in Chamberí

restaurants.name: La Bola
restaurants.review_quote: Open since 1868; specialises in cocido served in clay pots al estilo de antiguas tabernas de Madrid; one of the oldest cocido addresses in the capital

restaurants.name: La Capa
restaurants.review_quote: One of the toughest reservation assignments in the whole city; a new-wave southern Madrid address generating serious word-of-mouth

restaurants.name: La Casa del Abuelo
restaurants.review_quote: The most famous place in town for gambas al ajillo; a traditional tapas bar with a full restaurant menu that is equally fantastic

restaurants.name: La Castela
restaurants.review_quote: Built on a former Méntrida wine cellar in 1989; specialities include chipirones encebollados and garbanzos con langostinos

restaurants.name: La Charca Restaurante
restaurants.review_quote: Asgaya Group's fire-focused venue drawing on Asturian heritage with charcoal and wood smoke central to the cooking technique

restaurants.name: La Dolores
restaurants.review_quote: Downtown tavern celebrated for its Gilda — the olive, anchovy, and pickled pepper tapa combination that defines the bar snack tradition

restaurants.name: La Gran Tasca
restaurants.review_quote: Open since 1942 and considered one of the best places for cocido madrileño in the capital

restaurants.name: La Montaña
restaurants.review_quote: Asturian cuisine since 1943 featuring verdinas con carabineros, pepitoria, merluza rebozada, and fabada; deep northern cooking in central Madrid

restaurants.name: La Sanabresa
restaurants.review_quote: A true casa de comidas that feels like eating in someone's living room; the cocido madrileño served on Tuesday afternoons in season is unmissable

restaurants.name: La Trainera
restaurants.review_quote: Maritime-themed interior open since 1966; grilled turbot often said to be the best in Madrid

restaurants.name: Las Tortillas de Gabino
restaurants.review_quote: Michelin Bib Gourmand-recognised establishment honouring their grandfather's 1930s legacy with the iconic Velazqueña tortilla and seven additional variations

restaurants.name: Le Bistroman
restaurants.review_quote: Possibly Madrid's finest French restaurant; a Provençal journey featuring pissaladière, escargot, and bouillabaisse with classical execution

restaurants.name: Leña
restaurants.review_quote: Dani García's secondary steakhouse concept in the same Hesperia building as Smoked Room; takes the traditional asador into contemporary territory

restaurants.name: Los Galayos
restaurants.review_quote: Open since 1894 in the Plaza Mayor area; famous for cochinillo, chuletones, and guisos; historically the venue for the Generation of '27 poets' final dinner in 1936

restaurants.name: Mamá Charo
restaurants.address: Puesto 09, Mercado de San Fernando, Embajadores, 41
restaurants.review_quote: No-reservation market stall blending Asian, Latin, and family traditions; one of the market's most distinctive and personal cooking projects

restaurants.name: Manifesto 13
restaurants.review_quote: Nicholas and Mark Duncan's modern Italian reimagined through Peruvian chef Danitza Alpaca's lens; mestizo interpretations of classic Italian dishes

restaurants.name: Marmitón
restaurants.review_quote: Pablo Sánchez and Lalo Zarcero's modest restaurant using traditional fermentation, smoking, and pickling techniques; seasonal and local orientation throughout

restaurants.name: Mesón del Champiñón
restaurants.review_quote: Famous for grilled mushrooms stuffed with chorizo and garlic; always packed and described as quirky; order champiñones at the bar

restaurants.name: Noi
restaurants.review_quote: Gianni Pinto's unconventional Italian using Mediterranean product with technical creativity; tagliatelle with tuna ragù is a signature

restaurants.name: Norah
restaurants.review_quote: Mediterranean reinterpretation featuring smoked sardines, squid pasta, and warm design; a neighbourhood address that rewards return visits

restaurants.name: O'Caldiño
restaurants.review_quote: Galician cuisine emphasising seafood and shellfish since 1973; one of the most established Galician restaurants in the Salamanca neighbourhood

restaurants.name: OME
restaurants.review_quote: Gastronomy workshop with three spaces: a dining room, a 6-seat bar counter with 6-pass menu at €100, and a chef's table for 6 at €120 celebrating Mexican cuisine

restaurants.name: Palo Cortado
restaurants.review_quote: The perfect place for sherry lovers; specialises in sherry wines and holds frequent tastings to deepen knowledge of the wine

restaurants.name: Panamera Madrid
restaurants.review_quote: Family-run fusion kitchen blending traditional Spanish recipes with organic local produce and Latin American influences; dishes include hummus de cocido con torreznos and corvina con crema de huacatay

restaurants.name: Pez Tortilla
restaurants.review_quote: Casual joint for exploring the Spanish tortilla de patata debate; variations with caramelized peppers, ham, truffle, or goat cheese with drippy centres

restaurants.name: Playing Solo
restaurants.review_quote: Eight-seat counter near chef Luis Caballero; kaiseki-inspired seasonal menus at €115 long and €70 short with Nordic influences

restaurants.name: Pollos Sukao al Carbón
restaurants.review_quote: Known for excellent empanadas and charcoal-grilled chicken with crispy fries; a popular street-food style address in Usera

restaurants.name: Posada de la Villa
restaurants.review_quote: Traditional inn-style restaurant on the historic Cava Baja since 1980; classic roasted meats and traditional madrileño dishes

restaurants.name: Pucará Orígenes
restaurants.address: Plaza de la República del Ecuador, 2
restaurants.review_quote: Modern Peruvian with nikkei influences reinterpreting ceviches and fusion dishes at an affordable price for the neighbourhood

restaurants.name: Restaurante Amós
restaurants.review_quote: Chef Jesús Sánchez presenting northern Spanish flavours with a refined approach; highlights include ravioli de centollo and turbot

restaurants.name: Restaurante BAMMBAO
restaurants.review_quote: Chinese-Japanese fusion offering ebi fry and lingote de oro; described as a top recommendation requiring reservations in advance

restaurants.name: Restaurante Lao Tou
restaurants.review_quote: Famous for its fish head soup and exceptional spicy eggplant; reservations recommended for this popular Usera local

restaurants.name: Rocacho Valdebebas
restaurants.review_quote: Contemporary grill concept emphasising fuego, respeto al origen y excelencia; a fire-focused address in the north of the city

restaurants.name: Roostiq
restaurants.review_quote: Zoilo Álvarez's temple of torreznos, pizzas, and champagne open since 2018; organic Arévalo estate produce; laminated super-crispy pork belly is the signature

restaurants.name: Sala Cero
restaurants.review_quote: Miguel Bonet's anticipated project with panipuri aperitivo paired with a pisco-cava drink during a theatrical reveal at the opening of the meal

restaurants.name: Sanlúcar
restaurants.review_quote: One of my favourite Andalusian bars; specialises in sherry wines; Iberian pork dishes and cuttlefish stew are must-tries

restaurants.name: Sisapo
restaurants.review_quote: Michelin-recognised project by chef Alejandro Aguirre merging Spanish cuisine with Asian and South American touches and innovative presentations

restaurants.name: Taberna Almería
restaurants.review_quote: Popular La Latina bar for tostas — toasted bread with varied toppings; casual atmosphere popular with a younger local crowd

restaurants.name: Taberna Andaluza Sacromonte
restaurants.review_quote: Quality wines and creative tapas near Usera Metro; a family-run address bringing Andalusian hospitality to the neighbourhood

restaurants.name: Taberneros
restaurants.review_quote: Great wine list with delicious tapas having a Middle Eastern flair; a combination that sets it apart from standard Madrid wine bars

restaurants.name: Tetsu
restaurants.review_quote: Intimate teppanyaki for a maximum of 22 diners across six cooking stations; features wagyu and Galician sea urchin; lunch menu at €60

restaurants.name: TonTon
restaurants.review_quote: Paris-rooted modern bistro with chef Alice trained at Ritz Paris, Celler de Can Roca, and Septime; classic French techniques applied to seasonal market produce

restaurants.name: Traca
restaurants.review_quote: Barcelona team's Madrid debut with a festive, provocative concept designed around shareable dishes and convivial energy

restaurants.name: Tres por Cuatro
restaurants.review_quote: Álex Marugán's seasonality-focused philosophy with concise menu and daily suggestions; rossejat de raya and rabbit confit are recurring highlights

restaurants.name: Treze
restaurants.review_quote: Saúl Sanz's cinegetic specialities: rossejat de pato, roasted pigeon, wild boar medallions; seasonal game authenticity is the defining principle

restaurants.name: Trèsde
restaurants.review_quote: Minimal aesthetic with EPP (starter-main-dessert) format and seasonal adjustments throughout; artisanal bread integral to the experience

restaurants.name: Umaki Bar
restaurants.review_quote: Informal sushi counter inspired by Tokyo bars, blending Japanese technique with local spirit; an accessible address for quality sushi

restaurants.name: Zalacaín
restaurants.review_quote: Historic Madrid establishment reinvented; steak tartar prepared tableside and Wellington with elegant execution returning it to prominence

restaurants.name: Ëko Bistró
restaurants.review_quote: Franco-Spanish bistro honouring tradition with an extensive beverage programme featuring rare wines and sake alongside classic dishes
